items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program and launching outreach to shopping centers to get unlicensed businesses to comply. This could affect which businesses operate legally in local shopping areas and how the city enforces business regulations.

items.headline: New economic incentives being created for retail and small businesses
items.summary: The city is considering economic development incentives specifically designed to support retail, small businesses, and startups in Cupertino. This could influence where new businesses choose to locate and which existing businesses expand or stay in the area.

items.headline: New mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading. This project will add housing to the area, with details finalized through a development agreement.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4) to secure state funding for housing initiatives. This could bring additional resources for affordable housing and development projects.

items.headline: Santa Clara seeking input on how to spend HUD housing grants
items.summary: The city is accepting public comment on its draft plan for federal housing funds for fiscal year 2026-27. This is your chance to weigh in on how the city allocates money for affordable housing and related programs.

items.headline: City hosting community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program likely addresses parking for unhoused residents or vehicle dwellers, a growing concern in South Bay communities.
